task: R69 Shutter full range capability mapping closure

implemented: Replaced fixed Shutter tick mapping with activeFormat-based log-scale tick generation
Kept activeFormat minExposureDuration and maxExposureDuration as explicit ruler endpoints
Updated runtime Shutter quantization to preserve activeFormat boundaries
Kept R66 sanitized custom exposure clamp as final write safety

diagnosis.ui_tick_issue: Shutter UI ticks were primarily generated from a fixed canonical list, so device-specific ranges outside or between that list were not fully represented.
diagnosis.runtime_quantize_issue: Runtime duration quantization used a fixed 1/10000s step, which could truncate very fast activeFormat durations before the R66 clamp.
diagnosis.clamp_role: R66 clamp prevented unsafe writes but could not fix incomplete UI-to-runtime mapping.

active_format_range.refresh: updateShutterCapabilityState(with:) publishes min/max seconds whenever camera capability state refreshes

display.expected_alignment: UI tick duration and runtime target duration share the same activeFormat-backed range

risks: True-device validation is required to confirm that each lens activeFormat exposes and accepts the expected full duration range.
AVCaptureDevice may still round the applied exposureDuration internally; Debug logs should be compared against runtime readback on device if discrepancies remain.
